summary: Collection::watch doesn't use the Collection's type parameter
description: See https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1161

summary: Driver Human Flow OIDC test assumes refresh cannot fail in human flow
description: The spec actually requires the driver to attempt another callback if refresh fails. human_4_4 spec test should have a callback count of 3 not 2 by causing refresh to fail.

summary: Investigate using serde_path_to_error in deserialization code
description: The serde_path_to_error deserializer wrapper provides much more useful deserialization errors than serde's standard error messages. We should consider using it throughout the driver wherever we perform deserialization. We should also benchmark to make sure using this wrapper isn't a performance/memory usage hit. If it does make an impact, we could consider adding a feature flag for it. We've had many user complaints about serde's lack of detail in deserialization errors, so users might still want to use this wrapper even if it comes with a penalty.

summary: Driver benchmarks are broken
description: [Example run|https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/mongo_rust_driver_benchmarking_driver_benchmarks__topology~standalone_os~rhel90_dbx_perf_large_benchmark_driver_patch_a6d629fd8d70c15fc063c3c6909563a3a1821bca_669e84ef856eff000798e264_24_07_22_16_12_40/0/task?bookmarks=0,2179&selectedLineRange=L2079]; they're failing on server selection, so likely something has changed in orchestration setup since we last looked at this.

summary: cargo deny is failing
description: It looks like configuration format has migrated ([example failure|https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/mongo_rust_driver_lint_check_cargo_deny_patch_b90139ef019d3fe677549f32972ce4311a3b7054_66acf71f161ee7000711350d_24_08_02_15_11_28/0/task?bookmarks=0,1595]).

summary: Implement happy eyeballs for TCP connection
description: We currently prefer IPV4 addresses when establishing a TCP connection. This causes connection establishment delays when a network only supports IPV6 (see [this issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1170] for an example). We previously considered using happy eyeballs in RUST-257, but decided on the IPV4-first approach for simplicity and consistency with other drivers ([comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/blob/a6d629fd8d70c15fc063c3c6909563a3a1821bca/src/runtime/stream.rs#L90], [discussion|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/123#discussion_r377969026]). We should revisit this decision to improve connection times for use cases like the one in the linked issue. We should investigate whether there's already a suitable implementation of this in the ecosystem, e.g. the [happy_eyeballs|https://docs.rs/happy-eyeballs/latest/happy_eyeballs/] crate.

summary: Serverless tests are failing
description:
[Example run|https://spruce.mongodb.com/version/mongo_rust_driver_06eda2d62be894a33dd35d3ced34745736312733/tasks?sorts=STATUS%3AASC%3BBASE_STATUS%3ADESC].

Probably partially due to RUST-1991, but since both are failing I don't think that's all that's going on.

summary: Update version numbers before branching for release
description:
Right now, the relevant steps of our release procedure are:
* create new branch
* in new branch, update crate version number and first-party dependencies from git to crates.io
* after release, cherrypick crate version change back into main

This is a bit of a hassle; I propose that instead we:
* in main, update crate version number and first-party dependencies
* create new branch

The only wrinkle is that we want main to always use the git version of first-party dependencies; happily, cargo supports specifying [multiple locations|https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/specifying-dependencies.html#multiple-locations] for a dependency for exactly this use case, so we can just list both {{git}} and {{version}} for those and it'll work.

summary: Add a Utf8Lossy wrapper type for deserialization
description: This [user PR|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/1187] adds a feature flag to the driver to enable UTF-8 lossy deserialization, which the user needs to read invalid data inserted by a different driver. Doing so via the BSON library is currently inconvenient because UTF-8 lossy deserialization can only be enabled by calling one of the from_*_utf8_lossy methods, which doesn't play nicely with the driver's serde integration. Instead of exposing the driver feature flag, we should add a new Utf8Lossy wrapper type that users can embed their data types in to enable lossy deserialization. This would be similar to the existing HumanReadable wrapper type.

The actual events observed for failures always seems to be an empty array.

summary: Driver does not send endSessions on client shutdown
description:
Per the sessions spec the driver is required to send an {{endSessions}} command on client shutdown. However, we do not appear to currently do this.

[https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/sessions/driver-sessions.rst#endsessions]

Note that in our fix for this we should try to avoid issues like those described in NODE-4429 and JAVA-2859 where if a server is unavailable client shutdown hangs for 30 seconds until getting a server selection timeout error. Both Java and Node solve this by checking the topology first to see if there are any servers available matching a primaryPreferred read preference, and only attempting the command if such a server exists.

See: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/commit/5086eadf52e13369f584b9bd64477ab7f3522ff6] and [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-java-driver/commit/15210a62e9488b115b1be5f16c30b63bb2a3ef2c]

Also, note that once we fix this bug we will need to sync the CMAP logging test change made in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/1374.

summary: Remove derivative dependency

([https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_rust_driver_lint_check_cargo_deny_patch_57a6a70a3aaa22a4e915253f9c4977ca6f6a1514_673230259dbd090008b1ee45_24_11_11_16_26_14/0?type=T#L1448)] We should replace this dependency with another similar crate. derive-where and educe are both suggested alternatives.

summary: IPv6 server addresses are removed from the topology erroneously
description: Servers with IPv6 addresses may be erroneously removed from a topology due to an issue with string comparison. We should compare ServerAddresses directly rather than their string representations.

summary: Show chainable options in rustdoc for actions
description:
Right now, a user needs to know to click on the intermediate result type (e.g. {{Find}} in the signature for {{find}} [here|https://docs.rs/mongodb/latest/mongodb/struct.Collection.html#method.find]) to see what options can be set for the action.  This is very non-obvious, and we've had multiple user bug reports that boil down to this functionality not being discoverable.

We should list the option setters directly in the rustdoc for the action itself.

Connection tests were run locally, but we should add a test to CI. summary: Test compiling with no default features enabled
description:
We test compilation with default features enabled and all features enabled, but not with combinations beyond that. See here for an example of a compilation failure related to feature flag combination that we didn't catch in CI: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1291#event-16059818065. We should test more feature flag combinations - [cargo-hack|https://github.com/taiki-e/cargo-hack] might be useful for this.

Edit - updated ticket description to test with no default features enabled rather than combinations.

summary: endSessions on shutdown can outlive tokio runtime
description:
From https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1287, with 3.2.0 users have started seeing an error message pointing to {{Client::drop}}'s use of {{spawn}}.  Notably, this only seems to happen in test scenarios where Tokio runtimes and clients are being spun up and torn down repeatedly.

My current theory is that this is a recurrence of the issue we've seen repeatedly where a {{drop}} impl can race the destruction of the Tokio runtime and sometimes lose.

summary: OpenSSL is failing on Windows
description: It looks like it was broken by https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/1297; we should have noticed the [evergreen run|https://spruce.mongodb.com/version/mongo_rust_driver_b8d3127b0a335457525f4d008971b30ede749f6b/tasks?sorts=STATUS%3AASC%3BBASE_STATUS%3ADESC] for that PR failing.

summary: Use cargo-nextest's default filter
description: Cargo-nextest allows setting a [default filterset|https://nexte.st/docs/running/#running-a-subset-of-tests-by-default] to exclude some tests by default (using a nicely flexible DSL to specify which tests).  This filterset can be overridden by a command-line flag.  We should use this to replace our current use of environment variables to control test set selection for various environments/special circumstances.

summary: getMore cannot be executed when connected to a hidden node with directConnection=true
description:
See this user issue for the initial bug report: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1332

The crux of the problem is that getMore uses a SelectionCriteria::Predicate to select a specific server address, and when we evaluate predicates we also check whether the server is data-bearing, which excludes hidden members.

summary: bulk write result iteration is broken on load balanced topologies
description:
Example failures for various tests; the symptom is that a test will generally just wedge and eventually timeout:
test::bulk_write::failed_cursor_iteration: https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/mongo_rust_driver_load_balancer_test_load_balancer_latest_patch_509d27ea5b4e7a85928ef86802b940b156d8ad3c_677ecaae5d62220007e64203_25_01_08_18_57_51/0/task?bookmarks=0,2279
test::bulk_write::successful_cursor_iteration:
https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/mongo_rust_driver_load_balancer_test_load_balancer_latest_patch_509d27ea5b4e7a85928ef86802b940b156d8ad3c_677ff50b8a489f000785f55e_25_01_09_16_11_03/0/task?bookmarks=0,2144,2145
test::bulk_write::write_error_batches:
https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/mongo_rust_driver_load_balancer_test_load_balancer_latest_patch_509d27ea5b4e7a85928ef86802b940b156d8ad3c_6780002e45b6e500078e07cb_25_01_09_16_58_37/0/task?bookmarks=0,2191

Debugging shows that the problem is that {{get_connection}} for the {{getMore}} executing in the bulk write's {{handle_response_async}} never returns: it's waiting on the {{take_connection}} call in the first match arm (the op has a pinned connection), and that never returns a value.

Stepping back, from a systemic perspective, the problem is that:
* cursor operations executing on a load-balanced topology are required to be pinned
* when executed as part of {{handle_response_async}}, the connection is owned by the {{execute_operation_with_retry}} higher up the stack
* the {{getMore}} executed will attempt to fetch the pinned connection, which blocks until the previous holder drops it
* ... but that's {{execute_operation_with_retry}}, which won't drop it, it'll return it as part of the context of the completed operation
* ... so deadlock.

AFAICT this never worked for the specific combination of "bulk writes that required result iteration on load-balanced topologies", but because we were accidentally not running tests in load-balanced configuration we didn't notice until now.

summary: Check in Cargo.lock
description:
We currently don't check in {{Cargo.lock}} - this means that our CI breaks when downstream packages do and we have to maintain our own patch file to verify MSRV compatibility.  It's [generally|https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/faq.html#why-have-cargolock-in-version-control] [recommended|https://github.com/RustCrypto/formats/issues/1684#issuecomment-2691779873] to check in {{Cargo.lock}} to avoid these problems; we should consider moving to that.

If we do that, we'll also want to consider adding a new Evergreen task specifically to compile against latest version of dependencies to catch breakages in a controlled way; this would also go nicely with something like dependabot (RUST-557).
